subject: [For Review] Jan CB Monthly Management Meeting

content.answer:
Hi Ian/JH,

Please find attached deck for upcoming CB Management Meeting with Terence
for your review. A couple of slides WIP has yellow stickers, will be ready
by 20th Jan before 2nd review.

Let me know if you have any questions.

Target timeline to meeting as per below:

   - 1st Review Reply - 20th Jan 6pm SGT
   - 2nd Review Reply - 21st Jan, 6pm SGT
   - 3rd Review Reply (if needed) - 22nd Jan, 12pm SGT
   - Send to TP - 22nd Jan, Wed, 6pm SGT


-- 
*Jason YU*
*Regional CB*
*Mobile*: +65 90284825
*Email*: jason.yu@shopee.com

Hi JH,

Please find responses to questions below. Have also attached V2 of the deck
here.

Slide 51: Is there any reason why ADO% over platform increased but managed
ratio dropped (Jan vs Dec)? It means our managed CNLS grew less than the
unmanaged pool during Dec campaign season?
CNLS managed ratio dropped mainly as a number of previously managed shops
have dropped to unmanaged status during the local re-tiering process.
As seen from table below, managed shops no. remained relatively stable
while unmanaged shop no. grew from 42,653 to 43,154 from Nov to Dec’24.
(Note that the table on Slide 51 is based on Nov and Dec’24 full month
data). Specifically,

   -

   1,182 shops with 87k ADO moved from managed shop list to unmanaged,
   which is the main reason for the drop in managed ratio.
   -

      The majority of these shops come from TH (557 shops with 40k ADO). We
      have confirmed with TH local that these are the sellers who we have lost
      contact with, i.e.sellers who have been not been in contact for
at least 3
      months
      -

      Another 225 shops with 22k ADO come from PH, which we are pending
      Local seller performance team feedback. Will follow up and share findings
      once hearing back
      -

   With another 667 shops of 57k ADO moving from unmanaged to managed,
   overall ~30k net ADO moved from managed to unmanaged, roughly 1% of CNLS
   universe ADO, i.e. 1% drop in managed ratio.



Slide 66: Who is accountable for this? Local CB SBS warehouse manager or
Shaowen's Reg team?
The owner of this is Local WH Ops; They will plan for capacity using the
inbound forecast sent by CN LFF Ops on a weekly basis and approve to adjust
W+1 quota to cater to incoming demand spikes reasonably.

Slide 82: Have we considered the "removal of VAT" and "SPX discount"
factors for TW in this version?
Yes, we have considered the two factors and updated the data already in
this version. The main reason for

Slide 40

   be talking about how goals for this in the next few slides instead?
- How about Video?

Slide 63 – 67

   - I do not understand the content here very well



Slide 74

Slide 76

   - We should do TW – SG and TW – MY?
Should we cater 1.5 hours for this meeting instead?

1 hour was probably too
short from the last experience?

*From:* Jason Yu <jason.yu@shopee.com>
*Sent:* Monday, 20 January 2025 8:56 pm
*To:* Ian Ho <hoi@sea.com>; Jianghong Liu <jianghong.liu@shopee.com>;
Keljin Chua <keljin.chua@shopee.com>; Larry Lin <larry.lin@shopee.com>;
Cheng Derek <derek.cheng@shopee.com>; Leo Chen <leo.chenyh@shopee.com>;
Ruofei Wang <phyllis.wang@shopee.com>; Ning Li <ning.li@shopee.com>;
Chengchen Huo <chengchen.huo@shopee.com>; Shuning Wang <
shuning.wang@shopee.com>; Yuna Kwon <yuna.kwon@shopee.com>; Faye Yan <
faye.yan@shopee.com>; Jiahao Chen <jiahao.chen@shopee.com>; Jinyu Du <
jinyu.du@shopee.com>; Yiru Zhao <yiru.zhao@shopee.com>; Ray Wang <
ray.wang01@shopee.com>; Cuiwei Sun <cuiwei.sun@shopee.com>; Max Goh Shi
Long <max.gohsl@shopee.com>; Angela Hsu Yi-Han <angela.hsu@shopee.com>;
James Li [SZ] <james.li@shopee.com>; Shourou Li <shourou.li@shopee.com>
*Subject:* Re: [For Review] Jan CB Monthly Management Meeting



Hi JH,



Please find responses to questions below. Have also attached V2 of the deck
here.

Slide 51: Is there any reason why ADO% over platform increased but managed
ratio dropped (Jan vs Dec)?

It means our managed CNLS grew less than the
unmanaged pool during Dec campaign season?

CNLS managed ratio dropped mainly as a number of previously managed shops
have dropped to unmanaged status during the local re-tiering process.
As seen from table below, managed shops no. remained relatively stable
while unmanaged shop no. grew from 42,653 to 43,154 from Nov to Dec’24.
(Note that the table on Slide 51 is based on Nov and Dec’24 full month
data). Specifically,

   - 1,182 shops with 87k ADO moved from managed shop list to unmanaged,
   which is the main reason for the drop in managed ratio.


   - The majority of these shops come from TH (557 shops with 40k ADO). We
      have confirmed with TH local that these are the sellers who we have lost
      contact with, i.e.sellers who have been not been in contact for
at least 3
      months
      - Another 225 shops with 22k ADO come from PH, which we are pending
      Local seller performance team feedback. Will follow up and share findings
      once hearing back


   - With another 667 shops of 57k ADO moving from unmanaged to managed,
   overall ~30k net ADO moved from managed to unmanaged, roughly 1% of CNLS
   universe ADO, i.e. 1% drop in managed ratio.



Slide 66: Who is accountable for this?

Local CB SBS warehouse manager or
Shaowen's Reg team?

The owner of this is Local WH Ops; They will plan for capacity using the
inbound forecast sent by CN LFF Ops on a weekly basis and approve to adjust
W+1 quota to cater to incoming demand spikes reasonably.

Slide 82: Have we considered the "removal of VAT" and "SPX discount"
factors for TW in this version?

Yes, we have considered the two factors and updated the data already in
this version. The main reason for 1% YoY increase is due to XDD ramp up in
TW and XDD LM rate is the most expensive (45 NTD) in TW 店配 now.

Shourou Li <shourou.li@shopee.com> Could you add one slide to summary our
initiative under "Cost", "Speed", "Expansion" and "Long Term Capability" 4
pillars?

Please find responses below and attached v0.3 of the deck attached.


Slide 4

   -

   most recent submission?

Slide 20

   -

   Will we be updating the slides to reflect how we will treat CSPUs that
   have promised traffic vs that does not have?
   -

   Yes, updated in deck as page 22-23.



Slide 40

   -

   If we identify LFF + Livestream to be our key lever to grow, we should
   be talking about how goals for this in the next few slides instead?
   -

   How about Video?
   -

   Sure, added new pages 43-46 to show KR’s LFF targets, LFF growth
   drivers, LS target, Video targets


Slide 63 – 67

   -

   I do not understand the content here very well
   -

   As discussed offline, have included new slides 65-67 on IB forecast
   accuracy and utilization, as well as owners of the process; these are no
   regret, first steps to improving the inbound planning process


Slide 74 (Now 75)

   -

   This remains a slide that is hard to understand
   -

   Revised slides into the new iteration



Slide 76 (Now 77)

   -

   We should do TW – SG and TW – MY?
   -

   Yes. TW-SG and TW-MY proposed timeline per below table
   -

   Next step to update William in next TWCB meeting to confirm go/no-go
   decision for pilot and scale-up



Should we cater 1.5 hours for this meeting instead? 1 hour was probably too
short from the last experience?

Yes the meeting will start 30min earlier, future meetings will be 1.5hrs.
Have also invited Bingyu, Christin and Mohit
